content.problemStatement:
Daanish as always is busy creating and solving his favourite and interesting graph problems. Chef assigns each problem a difficulty — an integer between 111 and 101010. For each valid iii, there are AiA_iAi​ problems with difficulty iii.

A participant hacked Daanish's account and got access to the problem proposal document. He can delete up to KKK problems from the document in order to reduce the difficulty of the contest for himself and his friends. Find the smallest possible value of the difficulty of the most difficult problem which remains after removing KKK problems.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- One line with ten space‑separated integers A1 A2 … A10, where Ai is the number of problems with difficulty i (1 ≤ i ≤ 10).
- The next line contains a single integer K, the maximum number of problems that can be removed.
It is guaranteed that Σ Ai > 0 and 0 ≤ K < Σ Ai.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the minimum possible difficulty of the most difficult remaining problem after removing at most K problems.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we delete problems starting from difficulty 10 downwards until K is exhausted. The remaining highest difficulty is reported.
- Test 1: delete all 100 problems of difficulty 10, K becomes 0 → highest remaining difficulty is 8.
- Test 2: delete all 10 of difficulty 10 (K=2), then delete the single problem of difficulty 3 (K=1). The only remaining difficulty is 1.
- Test 3: K=0, so the highest difficulty present is 8.
- Test 4: delete all 10 problems of difficulty 10, K=0 → highest remaining difficulty is 3.
- Test 5: after removing 100 of difficulty 10 and then 23 more from lower difficulties, the highest remaining difficulty is 2.
K=1 allows removal of the single problem of difficulty 10. The next highest difficulty with a remaining problem is 9.
Remove the only problem of difficulty 5 (K=5), both problems of difficulty 4 (K=3), and three problems of difficulty 3 (K=0). The highest remaining difficulty is 2.

solution.approach:
For each test case we try to delete problems starting from the highest difficulty because removing a higher‑difficulty problem can only lower the final maximum difficulty. Iterate i from 10 down to 1:
- If K ≥ Ai, delete all Ai problems of difficulty i, set Ai = 0 and decrease K by Ai.
- Otherwise we cannot delete all problems of this difficulty, so we stop.
After the loop the answer is the largest i such that Ai > 0 (there is at least one problem left of that difficulty). The algorithm is O(10) per test case, i.e. O(T) overall, and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work (10 iterations) for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
